title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. comosa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. comosa, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its rosettes of rounded leaves and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of lady's mantle include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Lady's mantle can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat a variety of health conditions.
